title: SkeletonContext: Skeleton-side Context Prompt Learning for Zero-Shot Skeleton-based Action Recognition
problem_statement: Existing zero-shot skeleton-based action recognition (ZSSAR) methods struggle because skeleton data lacks contextual cues (e.g., objects), creating a semantic gap that hinders the discrimination of visually similar actions.
method_description: SkeletonContext is a framework that enriches skeletal motion representations with language-driven contextual knowledge. It works by first extracting action-related contextual descriptions (e.g., associated objects and environments) from Large Language Models (LLMs) and then injecting this reconstructed contextual semantics into the skeleton representations.
key_innovation: The reconstruction and injection of language-driven contextual semantics into skeleton representations to bridge the semantic gap and improve cross-modal alignment.
results_claims: The method achieves state-of-the-art performance in both conventional and generalized zero-shot settings, validating its effectiveness in reasoning about context and distinguishing fine-grained, visually similar actions.
